question.label: Support network
question.name: Who should be part of your go-to support circle here?
question.description: Identifies the relationships that should be intentionally built next to keep onboarding moving.

answer: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.

assessment.note: He names a concrete onboarding gap: practical handoff details are only learned during live incidents, signaling mild uncertainty and friction rather than a clear support network.

new_queue.label: Live incidents gap
new_queue.name: Those live incidents — which handoff details are still hardest to see ahead of time?

new_queue.label: Next support
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks easier for you?

history.answer_excerpt: I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.

I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
